---
title: "Copula"
author: "Guanyu"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  pdf_document:
    latex_engine: xelatex
  html_document: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Raw data

```{r cars}
library(tidyverse)
library(sf)

simd <- readr::read_csv("simd2020_withgeog/simd2020_withinds.csv")
simd_shp <- sf::st_read("simd2020_withgeog/sc_dz_11.shp")
simd_joined <- simd_shp %>%
  left_join(simd, by = c("DataZone" = "Data_Zone"))
joint_board_lookup <- tribble(
  ~Council_area,                          ~Joint_Board_area,
  "East Ayrshire",                       "Ayrshire",
  "North Ayrshire",                      "Ayrshire",
  "South Ayrshire",                      "Ayrshire",
  "Scottish Borders",                    "Borders",
  "Clackmannanshire",                    "Central Scotland",
  "Falkirk",                             "Central Scotland",
  "Stirling",                            "Central Scotland",
  "Dumfries and Galloway",               "Dumfries and Galloway",
  "Argyll and Bute",                     "Dunbartonshire and Argyll & Bute",
  "East Dunbartonshire",                 "Dunbartonshire and Argyll & Bute",
  "West Dunbartonshire",                 "Dunbartonshire and Argyll & Bute",
  "Fife",                                "Fife",
  "Aberdeen City",                       "Grampian",
  "Aberdeenshire",                       "Grampian",
  "Moray",                               "Grampian",
  "Glasgow City",                        "Glasgow",
  "Highland",                            "Highlands and Islands",
  "Na h-Eileanan an Iar",                "Highlands and Islands",
  "North Lanarkshire",                   "Lanarkshire",
  "South Lanarkshire",                   "Lanarkshire",
  "East Lothian",                        "Lothian",
  "City of Edinburgh",                   "Lothian",
  "Midlothian",                          "Lothian",
  "West Lothian",                        "Lothian",
  "Orkney Islands",                      "Orkney and Shetland",
  "Shetland Islands",                    "Orkney and Shetland",
  "East Renfrewshire",                   "Renfrewshire",
  "Inverclyde",                          "Renfrewshire",
  "Renfrewshire",                        "Renfrewshire",
  "Angus",                               "Tayside",
  "Dundee City",                         "Tayside",
  "Perth and Kinross",                   "Tayside"
)

simd_joined <- simd_joined %>%
  left_join(joint_board_lookup, by = c("Council_area" = "Council_area"))

# Pull out Lothian data

indicator_cols <- c(
"income_rate", "income_count",
"employment_rate", "employment_count",
"CIF", "ALCOHOL", "DRUG", "SMR", "DEPRESS",
"LBWT", "EMERG", "Attendance", "Attainment",
"no_qualifications", "not_participating", "University",
"crime_count", "crime_rate",
"overcrowded_count", "nocentralheating_count",
"overcrowded_rate", "nocentralheating_rate",
"drive_petrol", "drive_GP", "drive_post",
"drive_primary", "drive_retail", "drive_secondary",
"PT_GP", "PT_post", "PT_retail", "broadband"
)

simd_lothian_raw <- simd_joined %>%
  filter(Joint_Board_area == "Lothian") %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  select(DataZone, any_of(indicator_cols)) %>%
  mutate(
    across(
      where(is.character) & !matches("^DataZone$"), 
      ~ as.numeric(str_remove(.x, "%"))
      )
    )

na_summary <- simd_lothian_raw %>%
  summarise(
    across(
      all_of(indicator_cols),
      list(
        Missing_Count   = ~ sum(is.na(.)),
        Percent_Missing = ~ mean(is.na(.)) * 100
        ),
      .names = "{.col}.{.fn}")
    ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    everything(),
    names_to  = c("Indicator", ".value"),
    names_sep = "\\.") %>%
  arrange(desc(Missing_Count))


lothian_ind <- na_summary %>%
  filter(Percent_Missing <= 10) %>%
  pull(Indicator)

simd_lothian <- simd_lothian_raw %>%
  select(DataZone, all_of(lothian_ind)) %>%
  drop_na(all_of(lothian_ind))

# Standardize for PCA & clustering

lothian_data <- simd_lothian %>%
  select(-DataZone) %>%
  scale() %>%
  as.data.frame()

# Domain Mapping

indicator_domains <- tribble(
  ~Indicator,                ~Domain,
  # Income
  "income_rate",             "Income",
  "income_count",            "Income",

  # Employment
  "employment_rate",         "Employment",
  "employment_count",        "Employment",

  # Health
  "CIF",                     "Health",
  "ALCOHOL",                 "Health",
  "DRUG",                    "Health",
  "SMR",                     "Health",
  "DEPRESS",                 "Health",
  "LBWT",                    "Health",
  "EMERG",                   "Health",

  # Education
  "Attendance",              "Education",
  "Attainment",              "Education",
  "no_qualifications",       "Education",
  "not_participating",       "Education",
  "University",              "Education",

  # Access
  "drive_petrol",            "Access",
  "drive_GP",                "Access",
  "drive_post",              "Access",
  "drive_primary",           "Access",
  "drive_retail",            "Access",
  "drive_secondary",         "Access",
  "PT_GP",                   "Access",
  "PT_post",                 "Access",
  "PT_retail",               "Access",
  "broadband",               "Access",

  # Crime
  "crime_count",             "Crime",
  "crime_rate",              "Crime",

  # Housing
  "overcrowded_count",       "Housing",
  "nocentralheating_count",  "Housing",
  "overcrowded_rate",        "Housing",
  "nocentralheating_rate",   "Housing"
)

domain_vars <- indicator_domains %>%
  filter(Indicator %in% colnames(lothian_data)) %>%
  group_by(Domain) %>%
  summarise(vars = list(Indicator)) %>%
  deframe()


raw_ind <- indicator_domains %>%
  filter(Domain %in% c("Health", "Education", "Access")) %>%
  filter(!Indicator %in% c("Attendance")) %>%
  pull(Indicator)

rate_ind <- c("income_rate", "employment_rate", "crime_rate",
         "nocentralheating_rate", "overcrowded_rate")

raw_data <- lothian_data %>% select(all_of(c(raw_ind,rate_ind)))
```

```{r}
set.seed(123)
k <- 2
k_raw <- kmeans(raw_data, centers = k, nstart = 25)
lothian_data <- raw_data %>%
  mutate(
    cluster_k = k_raw$cluster
  )
```

## R-vine
```{r, fig.width=14, fig.height=6, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(rvinecopulib)
library(ggraph)

# independence test 

X <- lothian_data %>%
  select(where(is.numeric)) %>%
  select(-any_of("cluster_k")) %>%
  as.data.frame() # Keeps only the variables

X <- X %>% drop_na()

U <- pseudo_obs(as.matrix(X))

fit <- vinecop(
  data = U,
  family_set = "parametric",  # search common parametric families
  selcrit = "bic",            # penalizes complexity more than AIC
  tree_crit = "tau",          # dependence measure for structure selection
  trunc_lvl = Inf,            # fit all trees
  cores = 4                   # parallelize if possible
)

fit
summary(fit)
plot(fit)

logLik(fit)
AIC(fit)
BIC(fit)

```

```{r}
fit_cluster_vines <- function(df, cluster_col = "cluster_k", cores = 4) {
  clusters <- sort(unique(df[[cluster_col]]))

  res <- lapply(clusters, function(k) {
    Xk <- df %>%
      filter(.data[[cluster_col]] == k) %>%
      select(where(is.numeric)) %>%
      select(-any_of(cluster_col)) %>%
      drop_na() %>%
      as.data.frame()

    Uk <- pseudo_obs(as.matrix(Xk))

    fitk <- vinecop(
      data = Uk,
      family_set = "parametric",
      selcrit = "bic",
      tree_crit = "tau",
      trunc_lvl = Inf,
      cores = cores
    )

    list(k = k, X = Xk, U = Uk, fit = fitk)
  })

  names(res) <- paste0("cluster_", clusters)
  res
}

fits <- fit_cluster_vines(lothian_data, cores = 4)

edge_table <- function(fit) {
  sm <- summary(fit)  # data-frame-like

  vn <- attr(sm, "var_names")
  if (is.null(vn) || length(vn) == 0) vn <- paste0("V", seq_len(ncol(fit$data)))

  sm$var1 <- vn[sapply(sm$conditioned, `[[`, 1)]
  sm$var2 <- vn[sapply(sm$conditioned, `[[`, 2)]

  sm$cond_set <- sapply(sm$conditioning, function(idx) {
    idx <- idx[idx != 0]
    if (length(idx) == 0) "" else paste(vn[idx], collapse = ", ")
  })

  sm
}

edges_by_cluster <- lapply(fits, function(obj) edge_table(obj$fit))

edges_all <- bind_rows(lapply(names(edges_by_cluster), function(nm) {
  df <- edges_by_cluster[[nm]]
  df %>%
    mutate(
      cluster = nm,
      pair = paste(var1, var2, sep = " — "),
      abs_tau = abs(tau)
    )
}))

top_tree1 <- edges_all %>% filter(tree == 1) %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>% slice_max(abs_tau, n = 12, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()

ggplot(top_tree1, aes(x = abs_tau, y = reorder(pair, abs_tau))) +
  geom_col() +
  facet_wrap(~ cluster, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(x = "|Kendall's tau| (Tree 1)", y = NULL)
```

```{r, fig.width=14, fig.height=6, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(patchwork)

fit1 <- fits[["cluster_1"]]$fit
fit2 <- fits[["cluster_2"]]$fit

p1 <- plot(fit1, tree = 1, edge_labels = "tau") +
  ggplot2::ggtitle("Cluster 1 — Tree 1")

p2 <- plot(fit2, tree = 1, var_names = "legend", edge_labels = "tau") +
  ggplot2::ggtitle("Cluster 2 — Tree 1")

p1 | p2

```

```{r, fig.width=12, fig.height= 7.8, dpi=300}
# ============================================================
# Chunk: VineCopula cluster-wise RVine + CVine + readable plots
# ============================================================

library(tidyverse)
library(VineCopula)
library(stringr)

# ----------------------------
# 0) Helpers
# ----------------------------

make_U <- function(X) {
  U <- VineCopula::pobs(as.matrix(X))
  colnames(U) <- colnames(X)
  U
}

get_cluster_X <- function(df, k, cluster_col = "cluster_k") {
  df %>%
    dplyr::filter(.data[[cluster_col]] == k) %>%
    dplyr::select(where(is.numeric)) %>%
    dplyr::select(-any_of(cluster_col)) %>%
    tidyr::drop_na() %>%
    as.data.frame()
}

fit_one_cluster_vinecopula <- function(df, k,
                                       cluster_col = "cluster_k",
                                       type = c("RVine", "CVine"),
                                       selectioncrit = "BIC",
                                       treecrit = "tau",
                                       familyset = NA,
                                       trunclevel = NA,
                                       indeptest = TRUE,
                                       level = 0.05,
                                       cores = 4,
                                       progress = FALSE) {
  type <- match.arg(type)

  Xk <- get_cluster_X(df, k, cluster_col = cluster_col)
  Uk <- make_U(Xk)

  RVM <- VineCopula::RVineStructureSelect(
    data = Uk,
    familyset = familyset,
    type = type,                 # "RVine" or "CVine"
    selectioncrit = selectioncrit,
    indeptest = indeptest,
    level = level,
    trunclevel = trunclevel,
    treecrit = treecrit,
    cores = cores,
    progress = progress
  )

  # IMPORTANT: store variable names so plots + downstream extraction are readable
  RVM$names <- colnames(Uk)

  list(cluster = k, X = Xk, U = Uk, RVM = RVM)
}

fit_clusterwise_vines <- function(df,
                                  cluster_col = "cluster_k",
                                  clusters = NULL,
                                  type = c("RVine", "CVine"),
                                  selectioncrit = "BIC",
                                  indeptest = TRUE,
                                  level = 0.05,
                                  trunclevel = NA,
                                  familyset = NA,
                                  cores = 4) {
  type <- match.arg(type)
  if (is.null(clusters)) clusters <- sort(unique(df[[cluster_col]]))

  fits <- purrr::map(clusters, ~fit_one_cluster_vinecopula(
    df = df, k = .x, cluster_col = cluster_col,
    type = type,
    selectioncrit = selectioncrit,
    indeptest = indeptest,
    level = level,
    trunclevel = trunclevel,
    familyset = familyset,
    cores = cores
  ))

  names(fits) <- paste0("cluster_", clusters)
  fits
}

# create fits_rvine / fits_cvine (VineCopula)
fits_rvine <- fit_clusterwise_vines(
  df = lothian_data,
  cluster_col = "cluster_k",
  type = "RVine",
  selectioncrit = "BIC",
  indeptest = TRUE,
  level = 0.05,
  cores = 4
)

fits_cvine <- fit_clusterwise_vines(
  df = lothian_data,
  cluster_col = "cluster_k",
  type = "CVine",
  selectioncrit = "BIC",
  indeptest = TRUE,
  level = 0.05,
  cores = 4
)


# =========================================
# WHOLE COPY-PASTE READY VISUAL CHUNK
# - Tree plots side-by-side for cluster_1 vs cluster_2
# - Tree >= 2 shows conditioning variables as NO (node numbers), e.g. "| 1,23"
# - Tree 1 shows tau labels by default
# - tau = 0 edges are still drawn (light dashed)
# - A natural "NO → Variable" key table is shown under the plots
# =========================================

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(tibble)
  library(stringr)
  library(igraph)
  library(ggraph)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(patchwork)
  library(gridExtra)
  library(grid)
})

# ---------- helper: canonical parse + formatting ----------
format_cond_no <- function(cond_idx) {
  idx <- cond_idx[!is.na(cond_idx)]
  if (length(idx) == 0) return("")
  idx <- sort(unique(idx))
  paste0("| ", paste(idx, collapse = ","))
}

# ---------- Extract edges (incl. conditional set) from print(RVM) ----------
extract_tree_edges <- function(RVM, tree = 1, var_names = RVM$names) {

  out <- capture.output(print(RVM))

  i1 <- grep(paste0("^Tree\\s+", tree, "\\b"), out)[1]
  i2 <- grep(paste0("^Tree\\s+", tree + 1, "\\b"), out)[1]
  if (is.na(i1)) stop(paste0("Could not find 'Tree ", tree, "' in print(RVM)."))
  if (is.na(i2)) i2 <- length(out) + 1

  lines <- out[(i1 + 1):(i2 - 1)]
  lines <- trimws(gsub("^#+\\s*", "", lines))
  lines <- lines[nzchar(lines)]
  lines <- lines[grepl("^[0-9]+\\s*,\\s*[0-9]+", lines)]

  if (length(lines) == 0) {
    return(tibble(
      idx1 = integer(), idx2 = integer(),
      var1 = character(), var2 = character(),
      cond_idx = list(),
      family = character(), tau = numeric(), abs_tau = numeric()
    ))
  }

  toks <- strsplit(lines, "\\s+")
  pair_tok <- vapply(toks, function(z) if (length(z) >= 1) z[1] else NA_character_, character(1))
  fam_raw  <- vapply(toks, function(z) if (length(z) >= 2) z[2] else NA_character_, character(1))
  fam_raw[is.na(fam_raw)] <- "Indep"

  tau <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(str_match(lines, "tau\\s*=\\s*([-0-9.]+)")[, 2]))
  tau[is.na(tau)] <- 0

  # split "i,j;D" or "i,j|D"
  pair_part <- sub("[;|].*$", "", pair_tok)
  cond_part <- ifelse(grepl("[;|]", pair_tok), sub("^[^;|]+[;|]", "", pair_tok), "")

  # endpoints
  parts <- strsplit(pair_part, ",", fixed = TRUE)
  v1 <- trimws(vapply(parts, function(z) if (length(z) >= 1) z[1] else NA_character_, character(1)))
  v2 <- trimws(vapply(parts, function(z) if (length(z) >= 2) z[2] else NA_character_, character(1)))

  idx1 <- suppressWarnings(as.integer(v1))
  idx2 <- suppressWarnings(as.integer(v2))
  if (anyNA(idx1)) idx1[is.na(idx1)] <- match(v1[is.na(idx1)], var_names)
  if (anyNA(idx2)) idx2[is.na(idx2)] <- match(v2[is.na(idx2)], var_names)

  # conditioning set indices (list column)
  cond_idx <- lapply(cond_part, function(s) {
    s <- trimws(s)
    if (!nzchar(s)) return(integer(0))
    z <- trimws(unlist(strsplit(s, ",", fixed = TRUE)))
    z <- z[nzchar(z)]
    # usually numeric; fallback to name match if not
    zi <- suppressWarnings(as.integer(z))
    if (anyNA(zi)) zi[is.na(zi)] <- match(z[is.na(zi)], var_names)
    zi[!is.na(zi)]
  })

  tibble(
    idx1 = idx1,
    idx2 = idx2,
    var1 = var_names[idx1],
    var2 = var_names[idx2],
    cond_idx = cond_idx,
    family = fam_raw,
    tau = tau,
    abs_tau = abs(tau)
  ) %>% filter(!is.na(idx1), !is.na(idx2))
}

# ---------- Shared layout across cluster_1 & cluster_2 ----------
make_reference_layout <- function(RVM1, RVM2, tree = 1, seed = 1) {
  vn <- RVM1$names
  p  <- length(vn)
  nodes <- tibble(name = as.character(seq_len(p)))

  ed1 <- extract_tree_edges(RVM1, tree = tree, var_names = vn) %>%
    transmute(from = as.character(idx1), to = as.character(idx2))
  ed2 <- extract_tree_edges(RVM2, tree = tree, var_names = vn) %>%
    transmute(from = as.character(idx1), to = as.character(idx2))

  ed_u <- bind_rows(ed1, ed2) %>% distinct()
  g_ref <- igraph::graph_from_data_frame(ed_u, directed = FALSE, vertices = nodes)

  set.seed(seed)
  xy <- igraph::layout_with_fr(g_ref)
  tibble(name = igraph::V(g_ref)$name, x = xy[, 1], y = xy[, 2])
}

# ---------- NO → Variable key (more natural) ----------
node_key_grob <- function(var_names, blocks = 4, wrap_width = 16, fontsize = 8,
                          title = "Node number (NO) → variable") {
  p <- length(var_names)
  nrow <- ceiling(p / blocks)

  NO  <- as.character(seq_len(p))
  Var <- str_wrap(as.character(var_names), width = wrap_width)

  cols <- vector("list", length = 2 * blocks)
  coln <- character(2 * blocks)

  for (b in seq_len(blocks)) {
    i_start <- (b - 1) * nrow + 1
    i_end   <- min(b * nrow, p)
    take    <- i_start:i_end

    no_col  <- rep("", nrow)
    var_col <- rep("", nrow)

    if (length(take) > 0) {
      no_col[seq_along(take)]  <- NO[take]
      var_col[seq_along(take)] <- Var[take]
    }

    cols[[2*b - 1]] <- no_col
    cols[[2*b]]     <- var_col
    coln[2*b - 1] <- "NO"
    coln[2*b]     <- "Variable"
  }

  df <- as.data.frame(cols, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  names(df) <- coln

  tg <- gridExtra::tableGrob(
    df, rows = NULL,
    theme = gridExtra::ttheme_minimal(
      base_size = fontsize,
      core = list(
        fg_params = list(hjust = 0, x = 0.02),
        bg_params = list(fill = NA, col = NA)
      ),
      colhead = list(
        fg_params = list(fontface = "bold"),
        bg_params = list(fill = NA, col = NA)
      )
    )
  )

  gridExtra::arrangeGrob(
    grid::textGrob(title, x = 0, just = "left", gp = grid::gpar(fontface = "bold")),
    tg,
    heights = grid::unit.c(grid::unit(1.2, "lines"), grid::unit(1, "null"))
  )
}

# ---------- Tree plot: Tree>=2 default edge label is conditional NO ----------
plot_tree_ggraph <- function(RVM, tree = 1, title = "",
                             coords = NULL,
                             label_min_abs_tau = 0.0,
                             digits = 2,
                             label_zero_tau = FALSE,
                             edge_label = c("auto", "tau", "cond_no", "tau+cond_no", "none")) {

  edge_label <- match.arg(edge_label)
  if (edge_label == "auto") edge_label <- if (tree == 1) "tau" else "cond_no"

  vn <- RVM$names
  p  <- length(vn)
  nodes <- tibble(name = as.character(seq_len(p)), var = vn)

  ed <- extract_tree_edges(RVM, tree = tree, var_names = vn) %>%
    mutate(
      from = as.character(idx1),
      to   = as.character(idx2),
      lab_tau  = formatC(tau, format = "f", digits = digits),
      lab_cond = vapply(cond_idx, format_cond_no, character(1)),
      is_indep = abs(tau) < 1e-12,

      # ---- NEW LOGIC ----
      # If you're showing conditioning (cond_no or tau+cond_no), then
      # always show conditioning labels even for independence (dashed) edges.
      # Only apply abs-tau threshold to tau labels, not to conditioning labels.
      lab = case_when(
        edge_label == "none" ~ "",

        edge_label == "tau" ~ case_when(
          abs_tau < label_min_abs_tau ~ "",
          (!label_zero_tau & is_indep) ~ "",
          TRUE ~ lab_tau
        ),

        edge_label == "cond_no" ~ lab_cond,  # show for ALL edges, including dashed

        edge_label == "tau+cond_no" ~ case_when(
          # tau part only if strong enough (and not suppressed zeros)
          abs_tau >= label_min_abs_tau & (label_zero_tau | !is_indep) ~
            paste0(lab_tau, ifelse(lab_cond == "", "", paste0(" ", lab_cond))),
          # otherwise still show conditioning (even if tau small/zero)
          TRUE ~ lab_cond
        ),

        TRUE ~ ""
      )
    )

  g <- igraph::graph_from_data_frame(
    d = ed %>% select(from, to, family, tau, abs_tau, lab),
    directed = FALSE,
    vertices = nodes
  )

  if (is.null(coords)) {
    set.seed(1)
    xy <- igraph::layout_with_fr(g)
    coords <- tibble(name = igraph::V(g)$name, x = xy[, 1], y = xy[, 2])
  }

  ord <- match(igraph::V(g)$name, coords$name)
  x_v <- coords$x[ord]
  y_v <- coords$y[ord]

  edf <- igraph::as_data_frame(g, what = "edges") %>%
    mutate(
      x    = coords$x[match(from, coords$name)],
      y    = coords$y[match(from, coords$name)],
      xend = coords$x[match(to,   coords$name)],
      yend = coords$y[match(to,   coords$name)],
      xm   = (x + xend) / 2,
      ym   = (y + yend) / 2
    )

  ggraph(g, layout = "manual", x = x_v, y = y_v) +
    geom_segment(
      data = edf %>% filter(abs(tau) < 1e-12),
      aes(x = x, y = y, xend = xend, yend = yend),
      colour = "grey80", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.6, alpha = 0.9,
      inherit.aes = FALSE
    ) +
    geom_segment(
      data = edf %>% filter(abs(tau) >= 1e-12),
      aes(x = x, y = y, xend = xend, yend = yend, colour = family),
      linewidth = 0.85, alpha = 0.95,
      inherit.aes = FALSE
    ) +
    geom_label(
      data = edf %>% filter(lab != ""),
      aes(x = xm, y = ym, label = lab),
      size = 2.2, label.size = 0, fill = "white", alpha = 0.80,
      label.padding = unit(0.05, "lines"),
      inherit.aes = FALSE
    ) +
    geom_node_text(aes(label = name), size = 3) +
    scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = 0.06)) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = 0.06)) +
    coord_cartesian(clip = "on") +
    theme_void(base_size = 12) +
    ggtitle(title) +
    guides(colour = guide_legend(title = "Copula family")) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
          legend.position = "bottom")
}

# ---------- Side-by-side wrapper (with node key underneath) ----------
plot_tree_side_by_side <- function(fits, tree = 1, main_prefix = "",
                                   label_min_abs_tau = 0.0, digits = 2,
                                   show_node_key = TRUE,
                                   key_blocks = 3, key_wrap_width = 16, key_fontsize = 8,
                                   edge_label = "auto") {

  R1 <- fits[["cluster_1"]]$RVM
  R2 <- fits[["cluster_2"]]$RVM
  coords <- make_reference_layout(R1, R2, tree = tree, seed = 1)

  # sensible default thresholds: Tree 1 higher, Tree >= 2 lower
  if (is.null(label_min_abs_tau)) label_min_abs_tau <- if (tree == 1) 0.20 else 0.10

  p1 <- plot_tree_ggraph(R1, tree = tree,
                         title = paste0(main_prefix, "Cluster 1 - Tree ", tree),
                         coords = coords,
                         label_min_abs_tau = label_min_abs_tau,
                         digits = digits,
                         edge_label = edge_label)

  p2 <- plot_tree_ggraph(R2, tree = tree,
                         title = paste0(main_prefix, "Cluster 2 - Tree ", tree),
                         coords = coords,
                         label_min_abs_tau = label_min_abs_tau,
                         digits = digits,
                         edge_label = edge_label)

  top <- (p1 | p2) + plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")

  if (!show_node_key) return(top)

  key <- wrap_elements(
    full = node_key_grob(R1$names, blocks = key_blocks,
                         wrap_width = key_wrap_width, fontsize = key_fontsize,
                         title = paste0(main_prefix, "Node number (NO) → variable"))
  )

  (top / key) + plot_layout(heights = c(1, 0.38))
}

# ======================
# Example usage:
# ======================
# Tree 1 (tau labels):
plot_tree_side_by_side(fits_rvine, tree = 1, main_prefix = "RVine | ")
plot_tree_side_by_side(fits_rvine, tree = 2, main_prefix = "RVine | ",
                       edge_label = "tau+cond_no")
plot_tree_side_by_side(fits_cvine, tree = 1, main_prefix = "CVine | ")
plot_tree_side_by_side(fits_cvine, tree = 2, main_prefix = "CVine | ",
                       edge_label = "tau+cond_no")
```

```{r}
# =========================================
# Accurate bar charts: Cluster 1, Cluster 2, and Difference
# (robust to cluster naming and A-B vs B-A duplicates)
# =========================================

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)

# Canonicalise pair key so A—B and B—A are identical
canon_pair <- function(a, b, sep = " — ") {
  ifelse(a <= b, paste(a, b, sep = sep), paste(b, a, sep = sep))
}

# Normalise cluster labels to C1/C2 (robust)
norm_cluster <- function(x) {
  x0 <- tolower(trimws(as.character(x)))
  dplyr::case_when(
    x0 %in% c("cluster_1", "cluster 1", "c1", "1") ~ "C1",
    x0 %in% c("cluster_2", "cluster 2", "c2", "2") ~ "C2",
    TRUE ~ x  # fallback (shouldn't happen)
  )
}

# Build clean edge table for a given tree
build_tree_edges <- function(fits, tree = 1, wrap_width = 42) {
  purrr::imap_dfr(fits, function(obj, nm) {
    extract_tree_edges(obj$RVM, tree = tree, var_names = obj$RVM$names) %>%
      mutate(cluster_raw = nm)
  }) %>%
    mutate(
      cluster = norm_cluster(cluster_raw),
      pair_key = canon_pair(var1, var2),
      pair_lab = str_wrap(pair_key, width = wrap_width)
    ) %>%
    select(cluster, pair_key, pair_lab, tau, abs_tau) %>%
    distinct()
}

# Plot top-n for ONE cluster (separate chart)
plot_top_abs_tau_cluster <- function(edges, cluster = "C1",
                                     n = 12, title = NULL, digits = 2) {
  lab <- ifelse(cluster == "C1", "Cluster 1", "Cluster 2")

  df <- edges %>%
    filter(cluster == !!cluster) %>%
    # in case the same pair appears twice, keep the max |tau|
    group_by(pair_key, pair_lab) %>%
    summarise(
      tau = tau[which.max(abs_tau)],
      abs_tau = max(abs_tau),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    slice_max(abs_tau, n = n, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    arrange(abs_tau) %>%
    mutate(pair_lab = factor(pair_lab, levels = pair_lab))

  ggplot(df, aes(x = abs_tau, y = pair_lab)) +
    geom_col() +
    geom_text(aes(label = formatC(tau, format = "f", digits = digits)),
              hjust = -0.10, size = 3) +
    scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.18))) +
    labs(
      x = "|Kendall's tau|",
      y = NULL,
      title = title %||% paste0(lab, ": Top ", n, " edges by |tau|")
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
          axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9))
}

# Plot difference chart: Δ|tau| = |tau|_C2 - |tau|_C1
plot_top_abs_tau_absdiff <- function(edges, n = 12, title = NULL, digits = 2, wrap_width = 34) {

  wide <- edges %>%
    dplyr::select(cluster, pair_key, pair_lab, abs_tau) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    tidyr::pivot_wider(
      names_from  = cluster,
      values_from = abs_tau,
      values_fill = 0,
      names_prefix = "abs_tau_"
    )

  # safety: if any column is absent, create it as 0
  if (!"abs_tau_C1" %in% names(wide)) wide$abs_tau_C1 <- 0
  if (!"abs_tau_C2" %in% names(wide)) wide$abs_tau_C2 <- 0

  wide <- wide %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      absdiff  = abs(abs_tau_C2 - abs_tau_C1),
      pair_lab = stringr::str_wrap(pair_lab, width = wrap_width),
      lab      = formatC(absdiff, format = "f", digits = digits)
    ) %>%
    dplyr::slice_max(absdiff, n = n, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(absdiff) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(pair_lab = factor(pair_lab, levels = pair_lab))

  ggplot2::ggplot(wide, ggplot2::aes(x = absdiff, y = pair_lab)) +
    ggplot2::geom_col(width = 0.70) +
    ggplot2::geom_text(ggplot2::aes(label = lab), hjust = -0.10, size = 3) +
    ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(expand = ggplot2::expansion(mult = c(0, 0.18))) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      x = expression("|Δ| = ||tau|[C2] - |tau|[C1]|"),
      y = NULL,
      title = title %||% paste0("Top ", n, " edges by |Δ| in |tau|")
    ) +
    ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    ggplot2::theme(
      plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold"),
      axis.text.y = ggplot2::element_text(size = 9)
    )
}




# -------------------------
# Example usage:
tree_edges <- build_tree_edges(fits_rvine, tree = 1)

p_c1 <- plot_top_abs_tau_cluster(tree_edges, cluster = "C1", n = 12, title = "RVine: Cluster 1 (Tree 1)")
p_c2 <- plot_top_abs_tau_cluster(tree_edges, cluster = "C2", n = 12, title = "RVine: Cluster 2 (Tree 1)")
p_absdiff <- plot_top_abs_tau_absdiff(tree_edges, n = 12, title = "RVine: |Δ| (Tree 1)")

p_c1
p_c2
p_absdiff
```
```{r}
# =========================================
# CVine vs RVine comparison by cluster using logLik / AIC / BIC
# (fixes the NA issue by computing npar explicitly)
# Requires: fits_rvine, fits_cvine created earlier (each element has $U and $RVM)
# =========================================

library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(tibble)
library(VineCopula)
library(knitr)

# Count free parameters in a VineCopula RVineMatrix
# - 1 parameter for each non-independence pair-copula (family != 0)
# - +1 extra parameter whenever par2 is used (e.g., t, BB families), i.e., par2 != 0
count_vine_par <- function(RVM) {
  stopifnot(inherits(RVM, "RVineMatrix"))
  m <- lower.tri(RVM$family)
  fam  <- RVM$family[m]
  par2 <- RVM$par2[m]
  fam[is.na(fam)]   <- 0
  par2[is.na(par2)] <- 0
  sum(fam != 0) + sum(fam != 0 & abs(par2) > 1e-12)
}

# Robustly extract logLik from RVineLogLik()
get_rvine_loglik <- function(U, RVM) {
  out <- VineCopula::RVineLogLik(U, RVM)
  if (!is.null(out$loglik)) return(as.numeric(out$loglik))
  if (!is.null(out$logLik)) return(as.numeric(out$logLik))
  if (!is.null(out$value))  return(as.numeric(out$value))
  if (is.numeric(out) && length(out) >= 1) return(as.numeric(out[1]))
  stop("Could not extract log-likelihood from RVineLogLik().")
}

# One row (cluster, model) -> nobs, npar, logLik, AIC, BIC
ic_one <- function(fit, model_name) {
  U <- fit$U
  RVM <- fit$RVM
  n <- nrow(U)
  k <- count_vine_par(RVM)
  ll <- get_rvine_loglik(U, RVM)
  tibble(
    model = model_name,
    nobs  = n,
    npar  = k,
    logLik = ll,
    AIC = -2 * ll + 2 * k,
    BIC = -2 * ll + log(n) * k
  )
}

# Build table (same format as your screenshot, but with AIC/BIC filled)
ic_tbl <- bind_rows(
  imap_dfr(fits_cvine, ~ ic_one(.x, "CVine") %>% mutate(cluster = .y)),
  imap_dfr(fits_rvine, ~ ic_one(.x, "RVine") %>% mutate(cluster = .y))
) %>%
  mutate(
    cluster = gsub("^cluster_", "Cluster ", cluster),
    logLik = round(logLik, 2),
    AIC    = round(AIC, 2),
    BIC    = round(BIC, 2)
  ) %>%
  select(cluster, model, nobs, npar, logLik, AIC, BIC) %>%
  arrange(cluster, model)

kable(ic_tbl, caption = "CVine vs RVine comparison by cluster (logLik/AIC/BIC)")

# Optional: a compact “difference” table (negative means CVine is better = smaller)
ic_diff <- ic_tbl %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = model, values_from = c(logLik, AIC, BIC, npar)) %>%
  mutate(
    d_logLik = logLik_CVine - logLik_RVine,
    d_AIC    = AIC_CVine    - AIC_RVine,
    d_BIC    = BIC_CVine    - BIC_RVine
  ) %>%
  select(cluster, d_logLik, d_AIC, d_BIC)

kable(ic_diff, caption = "CVine − RVine differences (negative AIC/BIC => CVine preferred)")

```
